name: Kim Chi
recipeIngredient: 1 head Chinese cabbage, prepared as directed
3 whole red chili peppers, finely chopped
3 tablespoons fresh ginger root, finely chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the base off the cabbage.
2. Slice lengthwise into 6 segments.
3. Dry in the sun for half a day.
4. Cut each segment in half crossways.
5. Put the cabbage alternately with handfuls of salt and a sprinkling of cayenne pepper in an unglazed earthenware pot, making several layers.
6. Cover with a wooden lid that fits inside the pot directly on top of the cabbage.
7. Weight it down with a heavy stone.
8. Leave for one week.
9. Rinse the cabbage thoroughly under cold running water.
10. Squeeze out as much moisture as possible.
11. Slice into 2.5 cm (1 inch) sections or chop more finely if preferred.
12. Layer with onions, garlic, chilies, and ginger in a rinsed-out jar.
13. Fill the pot with Dashi stock mixed with soy sauce and MSG.
14. Cover with wax paper, put the lid back on top, and refrigerate.
15. After 4 or 5 days, the kim chi is ready for eating.
16. Serve with hot white rice and a dash of soy sauce.
Note: In cold weather, kim chi does not require refrigeration but should be stored in the refrigerator for up to 3 weeks when the weather is warm.
